name: Eggplant Parmigiana Alla Napoletana
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first eight ingredients in a bowl and heat over medium heat for about 20 minutes. Be careful not to scorch.
2. Reserve the sauce.
3. Slice the eggplant into half-inch slices, sprinkle lightly with salt, and let stand for about 20 minutes so bitter juice can run off. Rinse each slice and pat dry.
4. Coat each slice first with flour, then dip in beaten eggs, and finally coat lightly with bread crumbs.
5. Heat olive oil in a heavy skillet until it reaches half an inch deep. When hot, add the eggplant slices and cook for 2 minutes on each side or until soft and golden. Remove the slices and let them rest on paper towels to absorb excess oil.
6. Place about half of the reserved tomato sauce over the bottom of a baking dish sprayed with cooking spray. Arrange eggplant in layers, sprinkling each layer with cheeses and alternating until all ingredients are used, reserving a little cheese for topping.
7. Cover lightly with remaining sauce and reserved cheeses.
8. Bake at 325°F for 30 minutes.
